response.justification: Bogdan brings genuine staff-level pedigree and directly relevant AI-native engineering experience — his current Stripe role on MCP code generators and agentic workflow infrastructure maps cleanly to the role's baseline expectation that hands-on AI-driven development is table stakes, not a differentiator. Prior high-throughput microservices work at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic (millions of HTTP requests per second, async I/O, low-latency pipelines) demonstrates real distributed systems depth. The critical gap is Python: it is entirely absent from the skills list and from every role description across a 17-year career, yet the JD calls it a must-have. TypeScript coverage is solid. A short screen to surface undocumented Python exposure is warranted before a decision either way; advancing without it would require waiving a hard requirement.
strengths: Current Stripe role actively involves AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling — directly satisfying the JD's expectation that AI-native engineering is a baseline, not a bonus
Demonstrated high-throughput distributed systems experience at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic: millions of HTTP RPS, async I/O, fiber-based concurrency, low-latency data pipelines
Consistent staff-level scope across multiple companies with strong agile/TDD/CI-CD discipline and a track record of knowledge-sharing through workshops and community talks
concerns: Python is completely absent from the resume — skills section, role descriptions, and open-source projects all omit it — despite being a stated must-have technical skill
Distributed systems depth is dated (2018–2020); post-Sociomantic roles skew toward developer tooling, pricing logic, and GIS platforms rather than high-throughput data-path engineering
No explicit evidence of operational ownership: on-call rotations, incident response, production monitoring, or SLO accountability are not mentioned in any role
